purpose: Validate runtime visibility for Lane 3 verified SDXL/Flux LoRAs and validate an EC2-hosted self-hosted LLM route if local capacity is insufficient for AI_LLM_Intelligence_Plan target models.
commands_or_endpoints: Do not expose public unauthenticated LLM endpoints.

Under Lane 4 EC2 lease only: verify ComfyUI model visibility via the live ComfyUI `/object_info` endpoint, especially LoRA loader-visible names under `civitai_wave42/sdxl`, `civitai_wave42/flux`, and `wave42/sdxl`.

Under Lane 4 EC2 lease only: verify model path sync or mount coverage for `C:\Comfy_UI\Runtime_Data\models\loras\civitai_wave42` and `C:\Comfy_UI\Runtime_Data\models\loras\wave42` on the runtime host.

Under Lane 4 EC2 lease only: if the AI_LLM_Intelligence_Plan needs EC2 LLM capacity, validate an OpenAI-compatible self-hosted endpoint over SSM/SSH/private tunnel shape, not a public unauthenticated URL.
LLM endpoint check shape: `GET /v1/models` and one secret-free `POST /v1/chat/completions` sentinel request.
Preferred local default remains `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`; EC2 route should be fallback for GPU/server capacity and target Qwen3-Coder-class model validation.
expected_outputs: Evidence file with ComfyUI endpoint URL shape without credentials, endpoints checked, timestamp, and Lane_4 lease/cost state.
List of LoRA names visible to ComfyUI, grouped by SDXL vs Flux vs Wave42 existing SDXL.
Any missing model paths or relative-name mismatches, with exact next action.
If EC2 LLM is tested: model name, family, quantization/source manifest, endpoint URL shape without credentials, sample response, latency, and failure behavior.
Explicit statement that external providers are not required for normal AI_LLM_Intelligence_Plan operation.
safety_constraints: Lane_3 did not and must not start or stop EC2.
Do not print tokens, AWS credentials, Civitai URLs with credentials, SSH keys, presigned URLs, or provider API keys.
Do not mix SDXL LoRAs into Flux workflows or Flux LoRAs into SDXL workflows.
Do not download broad Civitai mirrors.
